title: find_biblical_parallel data payload
description: Find Mesopotamian textual parallels to a Hebrew Bible passage / theme / Mesopotamian source. Returns 15 canonical scholarly parallels (curated dataset, staged 2026-05-16) with named-Assyriologist attribution, transmission hypothesis, shared narrative elements, and pointers to the relevant brief in the cuneiform-research vault. Wrapped in StructuredEnvelope.

query.description: The lookup inputs the caller passed.

text.description: Canonical text name (e.g., 'Atra-ḫasīs Epic', 'Standard Babylonian Gilgamesh, Tablet XI').

summary.description: What the Mesopotamian text says + how it parallels the biblical passage.

brief_in_vault.description: Filename (without .md) of the cuneiform-research vault brief that discusses this source in fuller context. Pair with get_brief or query_research to drill down.

shared_elements.description: Specific narrative elements shared between the biblical passage and the Mesopotamian source(s).

confidence.description: Scholarly-consensus confidence: 'strong' for parallels with multi-decade established consensus (Flood, Creation, Theodicy), 'moderate' for parallels under active scholarly discussion (Song of Songs, Isaiah 14 fall, Healing Serpent).

transmission_hypothesis.description: How scholars hypothesize the parallel arose: scribal_transmission, common_substrate, common_topos_with_independent_developments, topical_critique_of_known_imperial_royal_ideology, direct_visual_environment_of_exilic_Judean, etc.
